rationale: The transferable engine (per hyp_abstract) is generating-function/branching-process recursions over a degree (functionality) distribution that locate a percolation-type critical threshold, which collapses to zero when the distribution's second moment diverges; the candidate applies exactly this engine, deriving the giant-component phase transition and its dependence on the (arbitrary) degree distribution via generating functions. However the candidate's actual object is graphs of the Internet, WWW, and social/collaboration networks — the same complex-network substrate the query paper (epidemic spreading on scale-free networks) itself studies, not the polymer-gelation object the hypothesis sought, so no genuine cross-field transfer has occurred.
